issuesFound.title: Missing page title

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks an aria-expanded attribute. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 41: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The button references aria-controls="solutions-submenu", but the target ID "solutions-submenu" is on a <ul> that is marked as hidden. While the ID exists, the button itself lacks the aria-expanded state to manage it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 53: <button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="solutions-submenu" aria-haspopup="true">

issuesFound.title: Landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <nav> elements on this page (main navigation and account navigation), but this instance lacks an aria-label to distinguish it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 101: <nav aria-label="Account">

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" is non-descriptive when taken out of context. Screen reader users navigating by links will not know what they are reading more about.
issuesFound.fix: Line 126: Read more about Luminary Studio features

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule sets outline to none. While it sets an outline-offset, it does not provide a visible replacement (like a non-zero outline, box-shadow, or border), leaving keyboard users with no visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: `  outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the focus indicator for elements that do not match :focus-visible without providing any alternative. This can block users of older browsers or specific assistive technologies that rely on standard :focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 342: `  outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on @media (forced-colors: active) :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: In high-contrast mode, the focus indicator is explicitly removed (outline: none). Users with low vision who rely on forced colors will have no way to identify the focused element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 348: `    outline: 2px solid CanvasText;`

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting word-spacing to -0.2em crowds text together, making it significantly harder for users with dyslexia or low vision to distinguish individual words.
issuesFound.fix: Line 186: `  word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting letter-spacing to -0.05em reduces the space between characters, which can cause letters to overlap or blur together for users with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 187: `  letter-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The explicit min-height of 20px is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum requirement of 24px for interactive targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 618: `  min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button is explicitly sized at 20px by 20px, which is too small for reliable activation by users with motor impairments or those using touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 631: `  min-width: 24px;`, Line 632: `  min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle height/width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: A size of 28px is below the recommended 44px target size and lacks sufficient surrounding spacing (8px) to meet the 24px target area requirement comfortably in all contexts.
issuesFound.fix: Line 671: `  min-height: 44px;`, Line 672: `  min-width: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height of 28px is difficult to target accurately on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 754: `  height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] height/width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button inside the search bar is only 28px square, making it a difficult target for users with limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition for movement, but there is no override in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block to disable this movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 425: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes a transition on 'transform', which triggers movement on hover/active states. This movement is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 590: `    transform var(--transition-fast);` (Add to @media block: `.btn { transition: none !important; }`)

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card transitions 'transform' on hover. While there is a transform: none override on line 976, the transition property itself still exists and can cause brief stuttering or movement.

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .stat-item

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global 'a' selector removes text-decoration. In body text (like within .prose or paragraphs), links will only be distinguishable by color, which fails WCAG SC 1.4.1 for users with color blindness.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle CSS classes and visibility but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the hamburger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation menu has been deployed or retracted.

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `play` and `pause` functions in the `testimonialsCarousel` module update the `aria-label` of the play/pause button but fail to update the `aria-pressed` state. This prevents screen reader users from determining the current state of the auto-rotation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2015: `_playing = true; setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true');`; Line 2024: `_playing = false; setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open(), close(), and toggle() functions in the mobileNav module change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the expanded state of the controlled menu.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav module, within the open() function, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');. In the close() function, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions change the visibility of dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 for interactive controls.
issuesFound.fix: In the _openInstance() function, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');. In the _closeInstance() function, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function expands an FAQ answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, while the close() function sets it to "false". This creates an inconsistent state for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In the faqAccordion open() function, add: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); immediately after item.open = true;.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the visible product count and grid but does not announce the change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: In the applyFilters() function, within the filterAnimation.run callback, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing period change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function changes the displayed prices and billing period but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users who rely on announcements will not know the billing period has changed.
issuesFound.fix: In the setPeriod() function, after updating the UI, add: liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function marks fields as invalid but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult() function, within the else block (when result.valid is false), add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function marks fields as valid but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. This may cause screen readers to incorrectly announce an invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult() function, within the if block (when result.valid is true), add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers do not have aria-expanded initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state of the dropdown panels.
issuesFound.fix: In the dropdownMenu register() function, after setting aria-haspopup and aria-controls, add: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not have aria-expanded initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state of the navigation drawer.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav init() function, after obtaining _trigger, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions change the visibility of the navigation menu but do not announce the state change to screen readers. Users who rely on announcements will not know if the menu opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav open() function, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);. In the close() function, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions change the visibility of dropdown menus but do not announce the state change to screen readers. Users who rely on announcements will not know if a dropdown opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: In the _openInstance() function, add: liveRegion.announce(`Dropdown menu opened`);. In the _closeInstance() function, add: liveRegion.announce(`Dropdown menu closed`);

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open`, `mobileNav.close`, and `mobileNav.toggle` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1060: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1075: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close), Line 1086: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, _open ? 'false' : 'true');` (inside toggle)

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function sets up the trigger and drawer but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` state. This leaves the trigger in an indeterminate state for assistive technology upon page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1115: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader._onScroll` function toggles the visibility of the header (hiding/showing it) based on scroll direction. This significant layout change is not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1358: `liveRegion.announce(movingDown ? 'Header hidden' : 'Header shown');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.play` and `testimonialsCarousel.pause` functions update the `aria-label` of the play/pause button but do not update the `aria-pressed` attribute to reflect the toggle state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1983: `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true');`, Line 1991: `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds a CSS class `CSS.fieldInvalid` and updates the error message, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2145: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function removes the invalid CSS class when a field becomes valid, but it does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2138: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm (min‑height 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` defines a minimum height of 20 px with only 6 px vertical padding, resulting in a total touch area smaller than the WCAG 44 px recommended size, making it hard for users with limited dexterity to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 460: `.btn-sm { min-height: 32px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon (min‑width 20px, min‑height 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-icon` sets a 20 px square with only 10 px padding, yielding an effective touch area of about 40 px, still below the 44 px minimum, which can cause activation errors for finger users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: `.btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` includes a `transition` for `transform`, causing spatial movement that does not respect users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a reduced‑motion override:  

  Line 380: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (translateY)
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state moves the primary CTA button with `transform: translateY(-1px)`, creating motion that lacks a reduced‑motion fallback, which can be disorienting for motion‑sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a reduced‑motion rule:  

  Line 250: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover (translateY & scale)

  Line 1320: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform transition)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions toggle CSS classes and the hidden attribute on the drawer and overlay but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the navigation menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1693: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1716: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger button. If the menu is closed on page load (default), the trigger must explicitly declare this state for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1765: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav overlay not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions manage the hidden attribute on the overlay element but do not manage aria-hidden. When the overlay is visually hidden, it should also have aria-hidden="true" to ensure it is ignored by assistive technologies.

Line 1698: _overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 1725: _overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus-visible sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the selector :focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator entirely in high-contrast mode where box-shadow is also suppressed.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn:focus-visible sets outline: none and only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator for buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for the search submit button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for account navigation links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for feature card links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: -3px, providing no visible focus indicator for FAQ accordion buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on form input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selectors .form-group input:focus-visible, select:focus-visible, and textarea:focus-visible set outline: none with outline-offset: 0, removing focus indicators from form fields.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on checkbox:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for checkboxes.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for footer navigation links.

issuesFound.explanation: The selector .social-links a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for social media links.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn-sm sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn-icon sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn sets min-height: 32px with vertical padding of only 7.5px (space-2-5 at 12px root font-size), resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44px without sufficient surrounding space.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .nav-toggle sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px with only 6px padding, below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on nav menu items
issuesFound.explanation: The selectors #nav-menu > li > a and #nav-menu > li > button set min-height: 28px with only 6px vertical padding, below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a sets min-height: 28px with only 7.5px vertical padding, below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #search-input sets height: 28px with only 6px vertical padding, below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"] sets width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .filter-tabs button sets min-height: 28px with only 6px vertical padding, below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .pricing-toggle button sets min-height: 28px with only 6px vertical padding, below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer nav links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a sets min-height: 24px with no padding compensation, below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .social-links a sets min-height: 28px with only 6px vertical padding, below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility renders content visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which renders the content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it available to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transform in its transition property, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 485 is empty and does not disable the transition.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link selector includes transform in its transition property, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition property, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 253: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets color: var(--colour-text-primary) (#555555) on background-color: var(--colour-bg-page) (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 4.3:1, which fails the WCAG 2.2 AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12: --colour-neutral-900: #333333;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary selector sets color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff) on background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.0:1, which fails the WCAG 2.2 AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text (16px semibold).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function toggles CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state of the navigation menu to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2169: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The button continues to announce as expanded after the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2193: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed via CSS classes, it is not removed from the accessibility tree with aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may still access the hidden navigation content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2180: Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` when closing. Line 2161: Add `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);` when opening.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function creates a focus trap reference but never calls _trap.activate(). Keyboard focus is not confined to the navigation drawer when open, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2170: Add `_trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the submenu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2298: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes, the function sets the hidden attribute on the panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2316: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they are given aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" for the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2273: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item opens, the function animates the panel and updates state, but unlike the close() function, it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4132: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded management
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox opens and closes suggestion panels but never sets aria-expanded on the input element. Screen readers cannot announce whether suggestions are visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3386: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` in _open(). Line 3393: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` in _close(). Line 3253: Add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` for initialization.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing aria-pressed on toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) visually indicate the active state via CSS classes but do not set aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3795: In _syncToggleState, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop.

issuesFound.title: productFilter _syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons indicate active state via CSS only. They function as toggle buttons but lack aria-pressed to communicate the active filter to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3564: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, it updates aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2848: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearing
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked as valid, the function removes aria-describedby references but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, leaving the field marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2868: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after clearing describedby.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed that filtering completed or how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3550: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` inside the filterAnimation callback after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods, prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3775: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` (or monthly equivalent) after updating the prices.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation changes the visible UI significantly but does not announce the state change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2170: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` in open(). Line 2193: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` in close().

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel auto-play missing pause mechanism check
issuesFound.explanation: While the carousel implements play/pause controls, the auto-play timer continues running in the background even when the carousel is not visible or when user interaction occurs, unless specifically paused. The pause on hover/focus is implemented but the setTimeout is not cleared until the callback executes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4715: Ensure `pause()` is called in `_onPointerDown` to stop auto-advance during user interaction (already present), but also verify the timer is cleared immediately when the page is hidden (visibilitychange listener recommended).
